response:
# Review verdict: hold for changes

Rubric I'm applying:
- **Block** — sev ≥ 0.7, **or** sev ≥ 0.5 with agent_risk ≥ 0.5 in a safety-critical area (billing/refund logic).
- **Warn** — sev ≥ 0.4 or agent_risk ≥ 0.5: worth fixing, not a merge blocker on its own.
- **Ignore** — sev < 0.3 and agent_risk < 0.5: noise on this PR; address opportunistically.

## Block merge (3)

| ID | Charge | sev / agent_risk | Why block |
|---|---|---|---|
| 00001 | God Function `generateInvoice` (204 LOC) | 0.90 / 0.63 | Only `high` finding. 3.4× the domain-function threshold inside billing. Future agent edits to one branch will silently miss interactions in another — exactly the failure mode `agent_risk` is meant to flag. |
| 00004 | Temporal Recklessness in `src/billing.ts` (7 clock reads) | 0.60 / 0.53 | Wall-clock reads scattered through invoice generation. Tests can't pin time without monkey-patching, and the file has no real assertions to fall back on. In billing this is invoice-correctness risk, not style. |
| 00008 | False Identity `calculateRefundPreview` | 0.55 / 0.52 | Name says pure preview, body calls `saveRefundAudit` + `sendRefundEmail`. Refund side effects under a "preview" verb is the kind of mislabelling that causes double-refunds when an agent calls it speculatively. Promoted to block on domain (refunds + audit trail). |

## Warn — fix before next release, don't block this merge (14)

Higher-impact warnings (sev ≥ 0.5 **or** agent_risk ≥ 0.55):

- **00002** Missing Agent Context (0.60 / 0.61) — repo-level, predates this PR; flag to follow up.
- **00005** Temporal Recklessness in `src/date.ts` (0.60 / 0.61) — utility module, lower blast than 00004 but same fix.
- **00007** "enterprise" literal in 4 files (0.50 / 0.60) — duplicated policy risk; centralise before a 5th copy lands.
- **00009** Concept Alias Drift (account/organisation/team/workspace) (0.55 / 0.58) — pick canonical term.
- **00010** God File `billing.ts` 310 LOC (0.60 / 0.50) — only 1.0× threshold; will resolve when 00001 is extracted.
- **00011** Logic in the Alibi: "Only owners can refund enterprise plans…" (0.50 / 0.50) — unencoded refund rule next to 00008. Encode as a guard.
- **00012** Duplicated Navigation Source `/settings/billing` (0.60 / 0.57) — two labels (Plans vs Billing).
- **00014** Route Metadata Drift `/settings/billing` (0.60 / 0.56) — 4 names for one destination.

Medium warnings (sev 0.35–0.45):

- **00006** Test with 0 assertions in `src/billing.test.ts` (0.45 / 0.46) — actively misleads; delete or assert. Borderline block given the billing context, but it's a single test.
- **00013** 14 TODO/FIXME in `src/todo.ts` (0.40 / 0.57) — 424/1k LOC is severe density; triage required even if individual markers are small.
- **00015** Broken doc link `docs/teams.md → ./setup.md` (0.35 / 0.51) — agents will follow stale instructions.
- **00021–00023** Orphaned routes `/account/subscription`, `/team`, `/workspace/members` (0.40 / 0.46 each) — three dead destinations cluster with 00009 (alias drift across the same surfaces). Could be in-flight work, but unlinked routes are typically half-finished refactors. Ask the author before merging which are intentional.

## Ignore on this PR (5)

| ID | Charge | sev / agent_risk |
|---|---|---|
| 00016 | Weak-matcher test "creates an invoice" | 0.25 / 0.34 |
| 00017 | Option Bag `describeCheckout` | 0.30 / 0.48 |
| 00018 | Return Shape Roulette `resolvePaymentResult` | 0.30 / 0.48 |
| 00019 | Negative Flag Maze (`!disableBilling && !skipRetry`) | 0.25 / 0.47 |
| 00020 | 4 TODOs in `billing.ts` (12.9/1k LOC, barely over threshold) | 0.20 / 0.40 |

All in `src/petty.ts` or just above noise floor. Worth a cleanup pass, not a review comment.

## Summary

**3 block / 14 warn / 5 ignore.** The blockers cluster in billing: one giant function (00001), wall-clock reads inside it (00004), and a misnamed refund function with side effects (00008). Recommend hold until those three are addressed; the rest can ship as a follow-up tracking issue.
